item.title: [LEGS-1340] Communities 2026
item.description:
Epic Goal

Drive Follow Goals of +5,000 new subscribers 7 day average daily run rate by the end of January 2026.

Background & Context

  • Board meeting success created mandate for follower/known user growth as primary 2026 KPI.
  • Early evidence of positive impacts to OPN and Traffic from Obituary Follow
    • How do we optimize this even more?

Success Metrics

  • Increase in daily/weekly new email subscribers
  • Growth in follower counts across person pages and communities
  • Improved conversion rates on follow CTAs
  • Enhanced mobile engagement and conversion rates

Key Initiatives & Projects

Quick Wins (High Impact, Low Effort)

  1. CTA Button Optimization
    • Replace "Submit" buttons with "Follow" across all pages
    • Test copy variations: "Follow" vs "Notify me" vs "Get alerts" vs "Get updates"
    • More descriptive messaging about what users receive
  2. New Person Page Follow Enhancement
    • Move follow CTA higher in user journey
    • Add bookmark icons to related memorial pages
    • Implement "Get notified when there are new [Name] obituaries" prominence

Search & Discovery Features

  1. Saved Search MVP
    • Leverage existing by-name stack for search result following
    • Example: Search "Matarangus" → follow Matarangus updates
    • Use first/last name from query, allow user confirmation
  2. Related Content Integration
    • Feed integration after "most recent batch" of obituaries
    • Custom cards for notable events and date-driven content
    • Cross-promotion suggestions (city page model achieved 20-24% CTR historically)

Newspaper Partner Integration

  1. Community and Notable Deaths Placement on Newspaper Pages
    • Enable community links on newspaper affiliate pages
    • Revenue share codes already in place
    • Coordinate with Ernie for partner approval
  2. Email Collection Enhancement
    • Dual opt-in: community signup + newspaper notifications
    • Auto-inclusion for relevant content
    • Affiliate attribution for co-branded site signups

Mobile & UX Optimization

  1. Mobile Follow Experience with Exit Intent Optimization
    • Address 70% mobile traffic with desktop-only CTAs issue
    • Mobile-optimized follow flows
    • Exit intent popups for leaving users
  2. Social Proof Integration
    • Add follower counts where available
    • Year-to-date signup data from Salesforce
    • Enhanced value proposition messaging beyond basic email alerts

Technical Infrastructure

  1. Follow Integration for Consumer Obituaries
    • TuKios integration for unique IDs and slugs
    • Enable community data integration

Marketing & Acquisition

  1. Facebook Advertising Campaign
    • Micro-community targeting (local vs broad)
    • 10-15 creative pieces, $10-15/day budget
    • Focus on biggest geographic markets for audience size

Dependencies & Blockers

  • Ernie approval for newspaper community cards
  • Braze vs Salesforce integration decision for consumer obits
  • Partner negotiations for email sharing and attribution
  • Engineering bandwidth allocation

Team Allocation

  • Priority shift: Focus next 2-3 weeks on follower growth over other priorities.
  • Engineering: Lemon bandwidth available for quick wins, cause Lemon Sllllaaaayyyysss. Along w/ Supporting cast from Man/Paul where necessary.

Success criteria

  • Users can update their passwords without issues.

Risks and dependencies

item.title: [LEGS-1219] Add Follow Options to Sign-In Flow
item.description:
Userstory: As a user, I want to select my follow preferences from a pre-selected list during account creation so that I can immediately start receiving relevant content without additional configuration steps.

Summary: Extend the "Sign in or Create an Account" process to include a step where users can choose their follow options (topics, categories, or preferences) from a curated list, improving initial personalization and engagement.

Context: Adding personalization during the onboarding process increases user engagement and reduces time-to-value. By allowing users to select interests or follow preferences during account creation, we can immediately tailor their experience and increase the likelihood of return visits.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • New step added to sign-up flow after account creation
  • Pre-selected list of follow options displayed to user
  • Users can select multiple options from the list
  • UI clearly indicates selected vs. unselected options
  • "Skip this step" option available for users who want to continue without selecting
  • Selected preferences saved to user profile/data model
  • Follow selections impact user's initial content/experience
  • Analytics tracking implemented for option selection rates
  • Flow works seamlessly with both email and social sign-up methods
  • Mobile-responsive design for follow options selection
  • Ability to modify follow preferences post-signup in account settings

Other information: Coordinate with content/product team to define the curated list of follow options. Consider future need for A/B testing the placement and presentation of this step for optimal conversion.
item.summary: Add Follow Options to Sign-In Flow

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: Add Follow Options to Sign-In Flow

item.title: [LEGS-1216] Extended Sign In Options with Google and Apple SSO
item.summary: Extended Sign In Options with Google and Apple SSO

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Userstory: As a user, I want to sign in using my Google or Apple account so that I can quickly create an account and access the platform without creating a new password.

Summary: Implement social sign-in functionality supporting Google and Apple authentication providers, allowing users to authenticate using their existing accounts from these platforms.

Context: Users increasingly expect social login options as they reduce friction in the sign-up process and eliminate the need to remember additional passwords. Supporting Google and Apple covers the majority of our target user base across both iOS and Android ecosystems.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • Google Sign-In integrated and functional across all supported browsers
  • Apple Sign-In integrated and functional (including "Sign in with Apple" requirements)
  • Users can create new accounts using Google or Apple credentials
  • Existing users can link Google/Apple accounts to their profiles
  • Email address from social providers properly captured and validated
  • Account linking handles edge cases (email already registered, etc.)
  • Privacy compliance met for both providers (data handling, consent)
  • Error handling for failed OAuth flows
  • Analytics tracking implemented for social login conversions
  • Fallback to email/password option always available

Other information: Requires OAuth 2.0 setup with Google Cloud Console and Apple Developer account. Review privacy policies to ensure compliance with provider requirements.
Extended Sign In Options with Google and Apple SSO

item.description:

Implement new password-based authentication flows for user sign in and sign up.

Context

item.title: [LEGS-1214] Apply Updated Designs to Auth Flows and My Account Pages
item.description:
Userstory: As a product team, we want to implement the updated designs for the account sign-up flow and my account page so that users experience a modern, cohesive interface that aligns with our current brand standards.

Summary: Update the POC version of the account sign-up flow and my account page to reflect the latest approved design specifications, ensuring visual consistency and improved user experience.

Context: The current POC version was built with preliminary designs. We now have finalized designs that incorporate user feedback, accessibility improvements, and brand guidelines. This epic covers the front-end implementation of these designs across the sign-up flow and account management interface.

Other information: Designs available in Figma - https://www.figma.com/design/0refXMT97US4WEm9Bk1Rj6/My-Account?node-id=1-8&t=L5HVi43EctOfqcBq-1 - Coordinate with design team for any clarifications or edge cases not covered in specs.

  • My Account page reflects updated design system components
  • Responsive design works across mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints

  • Design QA approval obtained before deployment

item.title: [LEGS-1200] My Account v1: My Communities
item.description:
1. Epic Description: My Account v1: My Communities ( LEGS-1200 Active )

The "My Account v1: My Communities" Epic aims to enhance user engagement and personalization within our platform by introducing features that improve the management of user connections and content discovery. This Epic focuses on delivering a more intuitive user experience, allowing users to save places and receive tailored recommendations, thereby fostering a sense of community and connection among users with similar interests.

The primary business objective of this Epic is to increase user retention and satisfaction by providing personalized content and streamlined subscription management. By implementing features such as personalized recommendations for saved places, relevant follow suggestions for new users, and a comprehensive subscription management interface, we aim to enhance the overall value proposition of our platform. This will not only drive user interaction but also encourage users to explore and invest more time in the communities they engage with.

Key deliverables within this Epic include the ability for users to edit their saved places, receive personalized recommendations, and manage their follow preferences seamlessly. The tasks involved in this Epic include: enhancing the saved places functionality to include personalized suggestions, introducing a follow recommendation system based on user behavior, and developing a user-friendly interface for managing followed pages. By focusing on these key areas, this Epic will significantly contribute to an enriched user experience and strengthen community ties within our platform.

item.title: [LEGS-1199] My Account v1: My Profile
item.description:
1. Epic Description: My Account v1: My Profile ( LEGS-1199 Active )

This Epic aims to enhance the user experience by enabling users to manage their personal information within the "My Account" section of our platform. Specifically, it focuses on allowing users to edit their basic profile details, such as name and phone number, and updating the account settings page to facilitate the management of personal information and sign-in methods. These enhancements are vital for ensuring that users can maintain accurate and up-to-date information, ultimately fostering trust and satisfaction with our service.

The business value of this Epic lies in empowering users to take control of their accounts, thereby improving user engagement and retention. By streamlining the process of updating personal information, we aim to reduce support inquiries related to account management and enhance overall user satisfaction. Key deliverables for this Epic include the implementation of an editable profile information section and an updated account settings page that reflects these changes, providing users with a seamless experience while managing their accounts.

The main components involved in this Epic include the following child tasks: the ability to edit basic profile information, which is currently in the "To Do" status, and the completed account settings page update, which has already been implemented by Wes Biffar. Together, these tasks will ensure that users have a comprehensive and user-friendly interface for managing their account details effectively.

item.title: [LEGS-1191] My Account v1: Login, Security, Observability
item.description:
Objective

Enhance the security and observability of the Customer Account CIAM system.

Context

This issue focuses on implementing logging, metrics, and alerting, as well as performing security hardening on the Auth0 configuration and application logic.

Scope

  • Included:
    • Implementing logging and metrics.
    • Setting up alerting via CloudWatch.
    • Performing security hardening on Auth0.
    • Enforcing security policies.
    • Conducting unit and integration tests.
    • Staging deployment and validation.
    • End-to-end UAT with stakeholders.
  • Not Included:
    • Changes to non-CIAM related systems.
    • Long-term monitoring beyond initial setup.

Success criteria

  • Successful implementation of logging and metrics.
  • Alerts are set up and functioning in CloudWatch.
  • Security hardening measures are enforced.
  • All unit and integration tests pass.
  • Successful staging deployment and validation.
  • Positive feedback from stakeholders after UAT.

Risks and dependencies

  • Potential delays in stakeholder availability for UAT.
  • Dependencies on existing Auth0 configurations and policies.

Other information

  • Error monitoring, performance baselines, and system health coverage are required.
  • Policies such as brute-force lockouts, session expiration, and email verification need to be enforced.
  • Final regression tests will be performed after addressing feedback from UAT.
item.summary: My Account v1: Login, Security, Observability

item.title: [LEGS-969] Braze SDK Integration - Legacy.com Components
item.description:
Objective

Integrate the Braze SDK with Legacy.com components to enhance campaign workflows currently managed by Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC).

Context

This initiative aims to establish Braze as the primary customer engagement platform for Legacy.com, replacing SFMC. It involves creating a modern data activation layer that connects various data sources for effective lifecycle messaging.

Scope

Included

  • Integrating the Braze SDK. https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/getting_started/sdk_overview
  • Implementing form-based workflows in Legacy codebases (Pandora, Pegasus, NextJS).
  • Identifying and implementing data bridges to push data to Braze.
  • Connecting to available APIs for CRM campaign workflows.
    • https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1g10HPeF5o2E3A7--XyJUVG5Dee-vIMZs/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=116345858056203829785&rtpof=true&sd=true
  • Documenting data use cases that cannot be solved with out-of-the-box tooling.

Not Included

  • Creating new Epics for gaps in data solutions.
  • Integration with other applications (Epi Server, iPublish, etc.).

Success criteria

  • Successful integration of the Braze SDK.
  • Data is pushed from user workflows to Braze.
  • All prioritized SFMC use cases are implemented.
  • Data bridges are established to facilitate data transfer to Braze.
  • Gaps in data are identified and documented.

Other information

  • Key Goals:
    1. Integrate SDK https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/getting_started/sdk_overview
    2. PUSH data to Braze from user workflows.
    3. Integrate data for Braze Use cases.
  • Linked Information:
    • Issue: https://legacycom.atlassian.net/browse/INIT-183
    • SFMC Workflows: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1g10HPeF5o2E3A7--XyJUVG5Dee-vIMZs/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=116345858056203829785&rtpof=true&sd=true
    • Key Deliverables include:
      • Implementation of Braze CRM and integration with <Legacy.com>
      • Data pipelines for synchronizing obituary, ecommerce, and user profile attributes into Braze

item.title: [LEGS-945] My Account v1: User Acceptance Testing
item.description:
Objective

The goal is to conduct User Acceptance Testing (UAT) for the My Account feature, which enables users to manage their obituaries through a seamless login experience and enhanced profile management.

Context

This initiative is part of the MyAccount MVP, which aims to improve the consumer account experience on Legacy.com by integrating Auth0 for authentication and providing a dashboard for managing obituaries. The project seeks to reduce customer support tickets and enhance user empowerment.

Scope

  • Included:
    • Testing the My Account feature for user login and obituary management.
    • Verification of the integration with Auth0 for authentication.
    • Evaluation of the user experience in accessing and managing obituaries.
  • Not Included:
    • Development of features beyond the MVP, such as direct editing within MyAccount.
    • Any changes to external partner systems at this stage.

Success criteria

  • Successful login via Auth0 without issues.
  • Users can view and manage their obituaries effectively.
  • Positive feedback from UAT participants regarding the user experience.

Risks and dependencies

  • Potential integration issues with Auth0 that could affect user login.
  • Dependencies on external partner systems for obituary management functionalities.
  • User resistance to adopting the new system if not intuitive.

Other information

  • The MyAccount MVP aims to provide a streamlined experience for users managing obituaries and is foundational for future developments.
item.summary: My Account v1: User Acceptance Testing

item.title: [INIT-230] AI Charity Attachment (CLIO + Pledge)
item.description:
Programmatically generate charity community pages at scale by integrating the Pledge nonprofit directory with Legacy’s group framework. Automatically associate relevant obituaries via AI-based charity extraction and matching to create searchable, sponsorship-ready charity communities.

Business Objective

Create a scalable, automated supply of charity community pages to unlock new sponsorship inventory, increase obituary engagement, and test SEO/referral lift via charity-linked content.

Problem Statement

Legacy currently relies on manual or partner-driven charity associations, limiting scale and speed. Without an automated charity group system, we miss opportunities for discoverability, engagement, and monetization tied to obituary-based charitable intent.

Value / Why This Matters

  • Creates 100K+ new indexed community pages with minimal marginal cost
  • Unlocks net-new sponsorship inventory for Sales
  • Improves reader engagement around charitable causes mentioned in obituaries
  • Strengthens Legacy’s data moat by connecting people → causes → communities

In Scope

  • Pledge API ingestion (1–1.5M charities)
  • CLIO partner exclusion logic
  • Charity extraction and matching from obituary text
  • Automated group creation and page rendering
  • Search indexing for charity groups and related obituaries
  • Sales-facing signals for high-priority charities

Out of Scope

  • Sponsor moderation dashboards
  • New UGC/comment systems
  • Email follow system changes
  • Historical obituary backfill (forward-looking only)

Success Metrics (KPIs)

Metric Target Date
Charity group pages live & indexed 100,000+ Mar 31, 2026
High-priority charities flagged (≥50 mentions) 500+ Mar 31, 2026
Baseline engagement captured Follows & page views tracked Mar 31, 2026

Primary Users / Stakeholders

  • Readers – discover and follow charities connected to obituaries
  • Sales – identify sponsorship-ready charity communities
  • Product – validate engagement and monetization hypotheses

Executive Sponsor

Antonio Esposito

Initiative Owner

Product Management – Legacy.com

Technical Owner

Legacy Technology – Search, Data, and Platform teams

High-Level Solution Approach

  1. Ingest charity data from Pledge into Legacy organization tables
  2. Exclude CLIO partners during ingestion and matching
  3. Use AI-based charity extraction on new obituaries
  4. Match charity mentions to Pledge orgs and persist associations
  5. Auto-create charity group pages with stable URLs
  6. Index groups and related obituaries into search
  7. Track engagement and surface high-value charities for Sales

Key Deliverables

  • Pledge API ingestion pipeline with refresh cadence
  • Charity exclusion registry (CLIO partners)
  • Charity extraction & matching logic
  • Automated charity group creation
  • Charity group page template and rendering
  • Search indexing (group + person updates)
  • Engagement and sales reporting outputs

Dependencies

  • Pledge API credentials and rate limits
  • CLIO extraction pipeline stability
  • person / person_keyword / person_groups tables
  • Group and person search index infrastructure
  • Indexing queues with batching and retries

Timeline / Phases

Phase Scope Target Window
Phase 1 Pledge ingestion + CLIO exclusion Jan 26 – Feb 12, 2026
Phase 2 Group creation + page rendering Feb 13 – Feb 27, 2026
Phase 3 Search integration + QA Feb 28 – Mar 14, 2026

status.description: This work item is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue: AI CLIO updates are stable going into Mid-March. Pledge Rollout to expand to all charities using AI extraction is in progress. Target date not confirmed yet as technical solutions being evaluated. Likely landing in Mid-April.

Risk Mitigation
Incomplete or inaccurate Pledge data Monthly refresh, observability, delta comparisons
CLIO partner overlap Authoritative exclusion list + automated audits
Indexing performance at scale Separate queues, debouncing, batch updates

item.title: [INIT-228] Intake Unification - Network Migration
item.description:
Intake System Unification will consolidate Legacy.com's fragmented obituary intake systems into a single, modern workflow based on Obitportal (front-end) and Adportal (delivery). This initiative will streamline internal operations, eliminate duplicate workflows, improve reporting and analytics, and provide a scalable platform that accelerates partner migrations and future feature delivery. The project sets the stage for a best-in-class, all-in-one partner and managed services platform, making Legacy.com the clear choice for operational excellence in the digital memorial market.

This initiative covers the consolidation of the Memoriams PPN product in to the Obitportla/Adportal, and the migration of the first POC batch of publciations.

status.description: This work item is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.

item.title: [INIT-227] AdPortal Integration POC - Tukios FH Website Admin Backend via Intake APIs
item.description:
Userstory: As a funeral home administrator, I want the AdPortal functionality integrated into the Tukios FH website admin backend via intake APIs, so that I can place obituaries directly for print and/or online publication efficiently, while establishing a replicable integration model for future platform partnerships.

Summary: Develop a proof-of-concept integration of AdPortal functionality into the Tukios funeral home website admin backend, creating a reference implementation and integration framework that can be replicated across other third-party platforms and partner systems.

Context: Funeral home administrators currently need to use separate systems to manage their website content and place obituary advertisements. By building a POC example of intake integration, we will not only solve this immediate need but also establish standardized intake APIs, integration patterns, and documentation that can serve as a blueprint for integrating AdPortal with other platforms (e.g., other funeral home management systems, CMS platforms, industry partners). This POC will validate our integration approach and create reusable components for future partnerships.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • AdPortal functionality embedded within a working POC
  • Reusable intake API framework developed and documented
  • Standard API endpoints created for obituary submission, status retrieval, and management
  • Integration architecture documented with diagrams and technical specifications
  • Authentication/authorization patterns established (SSO, API keys, OAuth)
  • Data mapping framework created for translating between platform formats
  • Funeral home administrators can create online obituaries from the POC
  • Print placement option available within the integrated workflow
  • Single sign-on (SSO) implemented between Tukios and AdPortal considered
  • Obituary data pre-populated from POC funeral home records where available
  • Real-time status updates visible in POC admin (submitted, approved, published)
  • Pricing and billing integrated with funeral home's existing payment methods
  • Order history accessible within POC admin interface
  • Error handling and validation consistent with standalone AdPortal experience
  • Performance requirements met (API response times under 2 seconds)
  • Comprehensive API documentation created for future integrations
  • Integration best practices guide developed
  • Reusable SDK or client library created (optional)
  • Code samples and integration templates documented
  • Testing framework established for validating future integrations
  • Security standards and compliance requirements documented
  • API versioning strategy defined for backward compatibility
  • Success metrics defined and measured for POC evaluation

Other information: This POC serves dual purposes: (1) immediate value for Tukios funeral home customers, and (2) establishing repeatable integration framework for future platforms. Document lessons learned and create integration playbook. Schedule review sessions with stakeholders to evaluate POC success and refine approach for subsequent integrations. Consider feedback from Tukios integration to improve API design before broader rollout.
item.summary: AdPortal Integration POC - Tukios FH Website Admin Backend via Intake APIs

status.description: This work item is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.

item.title: [INIT-226] Newspaper Partner Administration Tool for Non-iPublish Users
item.description:
Userstory: As a newspaper partner who does not use iPublish products, I want an easy-to-use administration tool so that I can access, manage, and place online obituaries efficiently.

Summary: Develop a standalone administration portal for newspaper partners not using iPublish, providing intuitive tools to access, review, edit, approve, and place online obituaries.

Context: Newspaper partners who don't use iPublish products currently lack efficient tools to manage online obituaries. A dedicated administration tool will streamline their workflow, reduce support requests, and improve partner satisfaction while maintaining quality control over published content.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • Standalone admin portal accessible via web browser
  • Secure authentication system for newspaper partner users
  • Dashboard displaying pending, approved, and published obituaries
  • Search and filter functionality (by date, status, funeral home, deceased name)
  • Obituary review interface with preview of online display
  • Edit capabilities for obituary content (with permissions/approval workflow)
  • Approval/rejection workflow with status tracking
  • Ability to schedule publication date/time
  • Bulk actions for managing multiple obituaries
  • User management for newspaper partner accounts (roles, permissions)
  • Activity log/audit trail for all actions taken
  • Notifications for new obituaries requiring review
  • Reporting capabilities (volume, status, trends)
  • Mobile-responsive design for on-the-go management
  • Help documentation and tooltips integrated into interface
  • Support contact mechanism built into tool
item.summary: Newspaper Partner Administration Tool for Non-iPublish Users

status.description: This work item is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.

item.title: [INIT-225] Online Obituary Creation with Print Upsell Option
item.description:
Userstory: As a user, I want to create an online obituary first and then have the option to add a print version through a print upsell option within the process, so that I can easily publish the obituary both online and in print.

Summary: Implement a workflow that allows users to create online obituaries with an integrated print upsell option, enabling seamless addition of print publication without restarting the process.

Context: Users often start with online obituary creation but may want to add print publication later in the process. By introducing a strategic upsell point, we can increase print revenue while maintaining a smooth user experience. This requires careful placement of the upsell offer to maximize conversion without disrupting the primary flow.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • Online obituary creation flow completed and functional
  • Print upsell option presented at optimal point(s) in the workflow
  • Clear messaging explains benefits and pricing of print publication
  • Users can easily add print option without losing online obituary progress
  • Print customization options available (size, placement, duration, newspaper selection)
  • Pricing calculation dynamic based on print selections
  • Order summary shows both online and print components with separate line items
  • Users can decline print option and continue with online-only publication
  • Ability to add print after completing online obituary (from confirmation page or account)
  • Payment flow handles combined online + print orders
  • Analytics tracking for upsell presentation, acceptance, and decline rates
  • A/B testing capability for upsell messaging and placement
  • Mobile-optimized upsell presentation

Other information: Coordinate with pricing team for print pricing model. Consider user research on optimal placement and messaging for upsell. Track conversion metrics to optimize over time.
item.summary: Online Obituary Creation with Print Upsell Option

item.title: [INIT-223] Automated Funeral Home Obituary Order Integration from Tukios and Legacy APIs
item.description:
Userstory: As a product manager, I want the intake system to systematically pull relevant Funeral Home Obituary orders from Tukios and Legacy APIs and automatically populate them in the relevant ad portal, so that we can work with out publisher partners to offer obit subscriptions products to Funeral Homes.

Summary: Build automated integration with Tukios and Legacy APIs to fetch funeral home obituary orders and populate them into the appropriate adportal, for publsihing on Legacy.com autoamtically as a newspaper obituary. This will power a new obit subscriptions product that our publisher partners want to offer to their Funeral Homes clients.

Context: Currently, funeral home obituary orders require manual entry into the ad portal, which is time-consuming, error-prone, and creates delays in publication. By integrating directly with Tukios and Legacy APIs, we can automate this workflow, reduce operational overhead, and improve turnaround times for obituary placement.

Acceptance Criteria:

  • Integration established with Tukios API for obituary order retrieval
  • Integration established with Legacy API for obituary order retrieval
  • System automatically polls APIs at defined intervals or uses webhooks for real-time updates
  • Obituary data accurately mapped from API responses to ad portal data model
  • Orders correctly routed to appropriate ad portal based on business rules
  • Duplicate order detection and handling implemented
  • Error handling for API failures, timeouts, and data validation issues
  • Logging and monitoring in place for integration health
  • Manual override/edit capability maintained for edge cases
  • Data transformation handles all required and optional fields
  • Integration supports both initial order creation and updates
  • Notification system alerts relevant stakeholders of new orders
  • Performance requirements met (processing time, API rate limits)

Other information: Coordinate with Tukios and Legacy teams for API documentation, authentication credentials, and rate limit information. Define SLA for order processing time.
item.summary: Automated Funeral Home Obituary Order Integration from Tukios and Legacy APIs

item.title: [INIT-194] Unified Newspaper Intake - Partner Platform
item.description:
This initiative delivers the Q4 2025 Newspaper Partner Platform Upgrade, a coordinated release that modernizes our newspaper intake capabilities, strengthens partner-facing functionality, and improves the reliability and performance of our publishing systems. The work included in this release consolidates enhancement requests, quality improvements, and targeted feature extensions that expand the platform’s capabilities and prepare it for higher intake volume, additional partners, and improved data fidelity.

The upgrade introduces improvements across integration pipelines, partner-specific configuration handling, intake logic, and administrative workflows. It also includes a set of platform refinements designed to reduce operational friction, improve partner satisfaction, and increase the stability and predictability of our newspaper publishing ecosystem. All enhancements, partner-driven features, and system-level optimizations for our newspaper network roll into this packaged release.

Business Value

The Q4 2025 upgrade provides a unified and predictable delivery vehicle for enhancements to our newspaper ecosystem, raising the baseline quality and capability of the platform. By bundling these improvements into a structured release, we simplify coordination with internal teams and external partners, reduce ad hoc disruptions, and ensure that meaningful advancements are deployed intentionally rather than incrementally.

This upgrade strengthens the overall intake pipeline, enhances partner reliability, and supports growth in obituary volume and partner engagement. It also sets the foundation for future extensibility and improved operational efficiency across the newspaper channel.

CapEx Justification (Platform Work – 5-Year Depreciation)

This initiative qualifies as Capital Expenditure because it delivers a coordinated quarterly upgrade to the Newspaper Partner Platform that enhances the long-term stability, scalability, and architectural capabilities of our newspaper intake systems. The improvements included in this release materially strengthen the underlying platform used across all newspaper partners, extend its useful life, and provide durable value beyond the immediate quarter.

The work modernizes core integration pathways, enhances intake and validation infrastructure, and improves the administrative tooling and processing logic that support all newspaper-driven obituary ingestion. These upgrades reduce operational overhead, expand the system’s capacity to support additional partners, and create a more resilient and extensible foundation for future roadmap features.

Because the majority of the work focuses on platform hardening, shared system enhancements, and architectural improvements rather than net-new end-user functionality, this initiative is classified as Platform Work (5-Year Depreciation) under Legacy’s CapEx guidelines.

status.description: This work item is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.

item.title: [INIT-183] Braze CRM Implementation & Data Activation
item.description:
This initiative introduces Braze as Legacy.com’s primary customer engagement platform, replacing Salesforce Marketing Cloud. The work establishes a modern data activation layer that connects obituary, ecommerce, and identity data into Braze for real-time lifecycle messaging. Integration requires coordinated development across the Unified Data Platform, obituary pipelines, and ecommerce systems to ensure data is standardized, timely, and extensible for multi-channel use.

Key deliverables include:

  • Implementation of Braze CRM and integration with Legacy.com data sources
  • Migration of existing campaigns and automations from Salesforce Marketing Cloud
  • Data pipelines to synchronize obituary, ecommerce, and user profile attributes into Braze
  • Standardized orchestration for email, SMS, and in-app messaging
  • Foundational reporting and attribution logic for campaign performance within Braze

CapEx Justification:

This initiative qualifies as Capital Expenditure – Platform Work (5-Year Depreciation) based on the following:

  • New System Foundation: Replaces legacy marketing automation with a durable, extensible platform for customer engagement.
  • Cross-System Integration: Requires coordinated development across data, ecommerce, and obituary systems to establish persistent pipelines.
  • Reusable Infrastructure: The integrations, data models, and orchestration logic will support ongoing campaigns and future products without rework.
  • Scalable Enhancements: Establishes a long-term foundation for lifecycle personalization, segmentation, and attribution across all Legacy.com channels.

This work meets the Legacy.com CapEx criteria by delivering durable platform enhancements that materially expand the company’s ability to engage customers and activate data at scale.

status.description: This work item is being actively worked on at the moment by the assignee.

item.title: [EDIRECT-899] Grow Followers - 2026 - Funeral Home eDirectory Followers/Communities
item.description:
1. Epic Description: Grow Followers - 2026 - Funeral Home eDirectory Followers/Communities ( EDIRECT-899 Done )

This Epic is focused on enhancing user engagement and community building within the Funeral Home eDirectory platform by implementing features that facilitate user followership. The primary goal is to grow the follower base for funeral homes listed on the platform, thereby increasing visibility and interaction. By encouraging users to follow specific funeral homes, we aim to create a more interconnected and supportive community, ultimately driving user retention and service utilization.

The scope of this Epic includes the development of several key features designed to prompt users to follow their preferred funeral homes. Notable deliverables include a user-friendly "Follow" modal positioned between obituary cards, an exit intent call-to-action (CTA) prompting users to "Stay in The Loop" on listing pages, and the integration of "Published In" icons on obituary cards to showcase related published data. These components will not only enhance the user experience but also provide valuable insights for funeral home owners regarding their audience engagement.

Main tasks associated with this Epic include the design and implementation of the "Follow" modal (currently in progress), the completed exit intent CTA, and the upcoming addition of "Published In" icons. Each of these elements contributes to the overarching objective of fostering a robust community around funeral homes, ultimately benefiting both users and service providers.
item.summary: Grow Followers - 2026 - Funeral Home eDirectory Followers/Communities

item.title: [ECOM-4433] Obituary Followers 2026 - Add Notify Me (Follow) to Ecom
item.description:
Epic Summary

Enable users to opt in to obituary-related notifications during the E-commerce checkout flow by introducing a Follow option. This allows customers to stay informed about updates to a loved one’s obituary while increasing overall engagement and traffic to Legacy pages.

Epic Description

Legacy currently provides the ability to Follow Obituaries and Communities to receive updates. Extending this functionality to the E-commerce checkout experience allows users to automatically follow an obituary associated with their purchase.

By adding a Follow option directly in the checkout email tooltip—and defaulting it to opt-in—users can seamlessly subscribe to obituary notifications. When selected, a GB Entries record will be created in the Legacy database to trigger standard obituary update notifications.

This capability improves user engagement, strengthens the connection to loved ones’ memorial pages, and drives additional visits to Obituary and Guestbook content through follow-up emails.
item.summary: Obituary Followers 2026 - Add Notify Me (Follow) to Ecom

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
Acceptance Criteria

1. Checkout Page Email Copy

  • Under the email input field on the Checkout page, copy updates to:
    “We’ll send order and obituary-related updates to this email.”

2. Email Tooltip Copy

Tooltip text is updated to include:

  • “An email address is required so we can send order and obituary-related updates.”
  • “Get email updates about [DECEASED NAME]'s page”

3. New Follow Opt-In Checkbox

  • A Follow opt-in checkbox is added to the email tooltip.
  • Checkbox is checked by default.

4. Database Behavior

If Follow checkbox remains checked:

A record is written to the GB Entries table with:

  • ReceiveNotifications = X
  • EntryType = F
    (Triggers obituary notifications as handled by existing Legacy DB logic.)

If Follow checkbox is unchecked:

A record is written to the guestbook table entry with:

  • ReceiveNotifications = NULL (or blank)
  • EntryType = F
    (No obituary notifications will be triggered.)

Business Value

  • Increases user awareness of obituary updates.
  • Enhances customer experience by providing valuable, relevant notifications.
  • Drives repeat engagement and traffic to obituary pages.
  • Ensures consistent follow functionality across Legacy products.

item.title: [ECOM-4418] Braze SDK Integration - EPI Server Components

Scope

Included

  • Implementing form-based workflows in Epi Server Codebase.
  • Identifying and implementing data bridges to push data to Braze.
  • Connecting to available APIs for CRM campaign workflows.
    • See https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1g10HPeF5o2E3A7--XyJUVG5Dee-vIMZs/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=116345858056203829785&rtpof=true&sd=true

Not Included

Success criteria

item.title: [DATA-413] Braze Integration - Migrate GB Entry Follows to Obit Follows (v2)
item.description:
Objective

The goal is to enhance the management of obituary follows by transitioning from the GB Entry table to a more scalable solution within Braze CRM. This initiative, termed “Obit Follow v2,” aims to streamline user preferences and ensure consistency by March.

Context

Currently, obituary follows are tracked using a GB Entry table, which is being phased out. The new system will allow for direct preference routing via SDK, improving data handling and user experience. Historical data must also be migrated to support existing user preferences.

Scope

  • Included:
    • Migration of historical data from GB Entry to new preference tables.
    • Implementation of SDK integration for direct preference routing.
  • Not Included:
    • Ongoing preference migration, as the SDK integration will handle future preferences.
    • Net-New integrations that were not previously part of SFMC workflows (See https://legacycom.atlassian.net/browse/DATA-424 )
    • Work captured in other epics related to SFMC migration to Braze

Success criteria

  • Successful migration of historical data to new tables.
  • SDK integration fully operational by the end of March.
  • User preferences correctly reflected in Braze CRM.

Data Migration and Setup Requirements

  • Data warehouse supports concept of user “follows” in a format that is consistent with how we store other “follows” preferences for users. (See https://legacycom.atlassian.net/browse/DATA-304?search_id=b2702346-789a-4693-b5f3-399e1565fa01 ) for context
  • Historic data from GB Entry tables has been migrated to new preference table(s) to support backfill.
  • Not needed: Ongoing preference migration because SDK integration will “fix-forward”

Migration Work Background:

We previously were determining an obit follow using a GB table entry. We are moving away from this moving forward. Summary of existing query source:

Existing (deprecated) definition of obit follower derived from:

FROM GB ENTRY DB TABLE*:

  • Email is not empty
  • ReceiveNotifications = X

 

SDK Integration  

OBIT FOLLOW PREFERENCE DATA POINTS (FUTURE STATE; SIMILAR TO LISTING PAGE CURRENT STATE)

  • See https://legacycom.atlassian.net/browse/LEGS-1115 for specifics on how this was resolved for other

Locations and ownership of the SDK Event --> Braze entry points and where this needs to be resolved via SDK:

Legacy:

  • Migrate from SFMC:
    • Left a GB Entry (and kept notifications opt in checked) - Legacy [LEGS]
    • Clicked “Follow Obituary” atop an obituary) - Legacy [LEGS]
    • Made an Ecom purchase (and kept notifications opt in checked) - Legacy [Ecom]
item.summary: Braze Integration - Migrate GB Entry Follows to Obit Follows (v2)

Context

The goal is to create a consistent way to store and manage user data. This includes profile information, preferences, and behavioral tracking data, ensuring integration with other system entities.

Scope

Not Included:

Success criteria

item.title: [DATA-328] Create Identity Model
item.description:
Userstory: As a developer, I want a well-defined identity model so that we can consistently manage user authentication, authorization, and identity data across the platform.

Summary: Design and implement a comprehensive identity model that defines how user identities are stored, managed, and referenced throughout the system, including support for multiple authentication methods.

Context: With the addition of social login providers and the evolution of our authentication system, we need a robust identity model that can handle multiple authentication methods (email/password, Google, Apple) while maintaining data integrity and supporting future identity providers. It should also be flexible for managing different Legacy Business Logins as collected here: https://legacycom.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/T2/pages/3546578963/User+Accounts

Acceptance Criteria:

  • TBC

Other information:

item.title: [DATA-313] Pledge Charity Ingestion & Matching Platform
item.description:
1. Epic Description: Pledge Charity Ingestion & Matching Platform ( DATA-313 Active )

The Pledge Charity Ingestion & Matching Platform Epic aims to enhance our charity matching capabilities by integrating pledge data into our existing systems. This initiative will enable more comprehensive charity extraction and matching processes, ultimately improving user engagement and satisfaction by providing a wider array of charitable options associated with obituaries. By streamlining the ingestion of pledge data from external APIs, we can ensure that our platform remains competitive and responsive to user needs.

The primary business objective of this Epic is to enhance data accuracy and availability in our charity matching process, which will lead to increased donations and better service for our clients. Key deliverables include the ingestion of pledge-related data, enhancements to our existing notice-queue and AI extraction models, and updates to the user interface to reflect these changes. By implementing these features, we will not only improve operational efficiency but also foster a more meaningful connection between users and charitable organizations.

The scope of this Epic encompasses several critical tasks, including updating the notice-queue for pledge support, sending pledge charity data to our vector database, and improving the functionality of our AI extraction processes. Additionally, the project will address user interface issues to ensure that the presentation of pledge charities is consistent and user-friendly. Through a collaborative effort from our development team, we aim to deliver a robust platform that optimally supports the charitable pledge ecosystem.

High-Level Solution Approach

  1. Ingest charity data from Pledge into Legacy organization tables
  2. Exclude CLIO partners during ingestion and matching
  3. Use AI-based charity extraction on new obituaries
  4. Match charity mentions to Pledge orgs and persist associations
  5. Auto-create charity group pages with stable URLs
  6. Index groups and related obituaries into search
  7. Track engagement and surface high-value charities for Sales

item.title: [DATA-281] DATA - Obit/Person Migration to Tukios - Legacy API & Data Updates
item.description:
Objective

The goal is to update the legacy API for the migration of data related to obituaries and persons to Tukios.

Context

This issue focuses on expanding the API to accommodate new community mappings, organization types, and various data requirements for different templates.

Scope

  • Included Work:
    • Community mapping and rooftop assignments
    • New group and organization types for person
    • Community data updates
    • Template-specific updates for Consumer, Celebrity, LPRO, and Affiliate
    • Ad and Partner configuration related to API data
  • Excluded Work:
    • Any changes not related to the API updates or the specific templates mentioned. (IE: Front-end updates)
    • Work owned by Tukios.

Success criteria

  • Successful implementation of community mapping and rooftop assignments.
  • New organization types for persons are correctly integrated.
  • Community data reflects the latest updates.
  • All specified links are generated on the pages.
  • Template updates are functional across all specified categories.
  • Ad targeting flags are correctly configured.

Risks and dependencies

  • Complexity in LPRO updates may require further discussion.
  • Dependency on Gannett's domain preferences and requirements for CNAME.
item.summary: DATA - Obit/Person Migration to Tukios - Legacy API & Data Updates

item.summary: Grow Followers - Data Extraction - Communities - Phase 4 - Bases

customfieldvalues.customfieldvalue:
As part of our program priorities to grow followers, we are expanding the scope of the communities that users can follow. To enable the new communities, work will be needed to extend our existing data extraction pipelines to extract different data types.

This initiative includes work done via AI Models, Obit-Intake workflows, API Updates, and other data-oriented technical work needed to expand our community data.

Military Communities:

Phase 4: Bases

Technical Implementation Pattern

  • Standard workflow for each attribute type:
    1. Data extraction and normalization rules
    2. Person attribute creation in database
    3. Organization/group creation and linking
    4. Group and search index updates
    5. API endpoint development
  • Stakeholder communication improved with progress visibility across all steps
  • LLM extraction preferred over keyword rules engine to avoid maintenance complexity

item.title: [DATA-254] Maintenance / KTLO (2026)
item.description:
1. Epic Description: DATA-254 Active - Maintenance / KTLO (2026)

The *DATA-254 - Maintenance / KTLO (2026)* Epic aims to enhance the operational reliability and functionality of our obituary intake system and related services. By addressing various logging, search validation, and performance issues, this Epic seeks to ensure that users can efficiently access and manage obituary data while maintaining a high level of service quality. The activities outlined under this Epic will directly contribute to improving user experience, reducing errors, and optimizing system performance.

This Epic encompasses a range of tasks designed to mitigate existing issues, such as logging Redis connection errors, resolving visibility problems of obituaries on search platforms, and implementing necessary infrastructure improvements. Key deliverables include enhancements to the obit-intake system, comprehensive documentation for internal teams, and the establishment of monitoring alerts to maintain service levels. The successful completion of this Epic will provide significant business value by minimizing operational disruptions and ensuring that our users have consistent access to critical obituary data.

The main components of this Epic include multiple child tasks addressing specific issues, such as the logging of connection errors, search clause validation, and the management of inactive users in our analytics platform. Each task is assigned to team members based on their expertise, ensuring a focused approach to resolving these challenges and enhancing overall system integrity. Through this initiative, we aim to uphold our commitment to delivering a reliable and efficient service to both our internal teams and external users.

item.title: [DATA-215] Grow Followers - Data Extraction - Communities - Notable Deaths (Interesting People & Stories)
item.description:
As part of our program priorities to grow followers, we are expanding the scope of the communities that users can follow. To enable the new communities, work will be needed to extend our existing data extraction pipelines to extract different data types.

This initiative includes work done via AI Models, Obit-Intake workflows, API Updates, and other data-oriented technical work needed to expand our community data.

This Epic includes data extraction rules for Notable Deaths - Interesting People and Stories.

Antonio Esposito - will fill in details on requirements for “Notable Deaths” and what qualifies for this data type.

Stephen Segal - Will provide current prompt used for identifying interesting people.

Requirements:

  • Currently the content team manually creates notable deaths in legacydb and publishes these obituaries live.
  • We want to place these people into a group/organization so that they can be displayed in a group page and be elligible for email subscriptions/etc.
  • /groups/notable-deaths/tbd

Technical Implementation Pattern

  • Standard workflow for each attribute type:
    1. Data extraction and normalization rules
    2. Person attribute creation in database
    3. Organization/group creation and linking
    4. Group and search index updates
    5. API endpoint development
  • Stakeholder communication improved with progress visibility across all steps
  • LLM extraction preferred over keyword rules engine to avoid maintenance complexity

Technical Notes

item.title: [DATA-123] SFMC to Braze Data Migration Work
item.description:
Objective

In order to retire SFMC, work needs to be done to migrate data from SFMC to Consumer Data Warehouse so that any CRM (IE Braze) can access historic SFMC data.

Scope

Included

  • Review and define scope of data needed for moving off SFMC
    • Determine what data can be dropped (IE redundant Obit data)
    • Determine how to access and store important CRM data
  • Figure out how to “back up” data in some way so that it can be retrieved or remapped later after SFMC is offline

Not Included

  • Setting up braze data within Braze

Success criteria

  • Successfully moved historical CRM-necessary data to CDW
item.summary: SFMC to Braze Data Migration Work

item.title: [DATA-95] Redshift - Braze (CRM) integration - Data Tables & Views
item.description:
Includes work needed to connect Braze with data warehouse for various data needs:

  • Unified user data
  • Unified obit data
  • “Connected Content” use cases (content for outbound Braze Campaigns)
  • Segmentation and audience data (Canvas)
  • Sync data for user data tracking from Braze to Data Warehouse

Scope Included:

  • Known tables outlined in https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HWFxK3APueiM1JlgTlshiJOM4HSAO1mybABAIT741Z8/edit?tab=t.0
  • Use case to support follow functionality as in Reference Doc with requirements: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xgjfrxQnrQbeI8BWaMAhw6Vygcgi738tzbhufpZeTRk/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.xsnoyi6ate8l

Not In scope:

  • Work already completed in https://legacycom.atlassian.net/browse/BI-942 for follows campaign
  • Other backfill work scoped in https://legacycom.atlassian.net/browse/DATA-94

item.title: [DATA-92] Braze (CRM) Preference Center - Redshift Data Modeling and Views
item.description:
Reference Doc with requirements: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xgjfrxQnrQbeI8BWaMAhw6Vygcgi738tzbhufpZeTRk/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.xsnoyi6ate8l

NOTE: “API & Transactional Data Layer” = not in scope for this ticket. Future state only.

Redshift Data Modeling & Views

See https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xgjfrxQnrQbeI8BWaMAhw6Vygcgi738tzbhufpZeTRk/edit?tab=t.0 for full requirements

Goal: Prepare Redshift to support Braze Currents, CDI, and daily obit matching.

Stories

  1. Create Master Follow Preferences Tables
    • Tables for: (See: https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HWFxK3APueiM1JlgTlshiJOM4HSAO1mybABAIT741Z8/edit?tab=t.0 )
      • Community
      • Local Market
      • Newspaper
      • By Name
      • Person Follow
    • Acceptance: schema supports nested attribute export (REQ_016–REQ_017)

Braze Currents → Redshift Integration Support

Goal: Ensure follow events land correctly in Redshift for preference reconciliation.

See https://docs.google.com/document/d/1xgjfrxQnrQbeI8BWaMAhw6Vygcgi738tzbhufpZeTRk/edit?tab=t.0 for full requirements

Stories

  1. Validate Currents Event Ingestion Schema
    • Ensure users.behaviors.CustomEvent table exists and is queryable (https://docs.google.com/document/d/1HWFxK3APueiM1JlgTlshiJOM4HSAO1mybABAIT741Z8/edit?tab=t.0 )
    • Sample Data: 
      users.behaviors.CustomEvent {
 "ad_id" (string)
 "ad_id_type"  (string) 
 "ad_tracking_enabled" (boolean) 
 "app_group_id" (string) 
 "app_id" (string) 
 "device_id" (string) 
 "device_model" (string)
 "external_user_id" (string) 
 "id"  (string) 
 "name" (string) 
 "os_version" (string)
 "platform" (string) 
 "properties"  (string) 
 "time" (int) 
 "timezone" (string) 
 "user_id" (string)
    }
    • Acceptance: follow events visible in Redshift (page 12) Legacy.com Follow Campaign Conc…
  2. Build Preference Reconciliation Logic
    • Merge Currents events into master preference tables
    • Handle activate/deactivate transitions
    • Acceptance: Redshift reflects latest follow state

Monitoring, Safeguards & Notifications

Goal: Reduce risk of bad sends and data drift.

Stories

  1. CDI Sync Monitoring Hooks
    • Detect failed daily catalog syncs
    • Notify Legacy stakeholders
    • Acceptance: alert fires on failure (REQ_013)
  2. Data Freshness Flags
    • Track last successful daily obit update timestamp
    • Acceptance: downstream systems can suppress stale sends
item.summary: Braze (CRM) Preference Center - Redshift Data Modeling and Views

item.description:

QA, Testing & Cutover Support

Stories

item.title: [BLUE-925] Intake Unification - HP - 2026
item.description: Intake Unification - Holding Pen. Temporary epic, while we sort out the Jira structure for the Intake Unification project.

item.title: [BLUE-923] Obitportal: Admin Enhancements - 2026
item.description:
This epic extends ongoing improvements to the Obitportal admin and transactional systems with a focus on customer identity, notification flows, link tracking, and emerging payment methods. Work includes backend updates and research spikes to guide future feature implementation.

In-Scope Deliverables:

  • Update Stripe integration to better manage customer metadata, improving traceability and platform reporting.
  • Add tracking to the funeral home link in confirmation emails for analytics and performance optimization.
  • Remove legacy notification logic for E-Edition publishing, enabling more maintainable future-state messaging.
  • Conduct research spikes on:
    • Klarna payment integration as a flexible consumer payment option.
    • Linking funeral home information with Legacy’s Pythia data model to support future CRM and analytics alignment.

CapEx Justification

This is Feature Work under GAAP ASC 350-40. The deliverables either enhance user-facing functionality or support foundational research for future product development. All changes are scoped to benefit ongoing product evolution across multiple partners and use cases.

item.title: [BLUE-922] Adpay Maintenance/KTLO 2026
item.description:
1. Epic Description: Adpay Maintenance/KTLO 2026 ( BLUE-922 Active )

The *Adpay Maintenance/KTLO 2026* Epic aims to ensure the continued functionality and reliability of the Adpay platform by addressing critical maintenance tasks and enhancements. This initiative encompasses a series of audits, updates, and optimizations that are essential for maintaining operational efficiency, compliance, and user satisfaction. By systematically tackling these tasks, our goal is to enhance the user experience, streamline processes, and mitigate potential issues that may arise from outdated configurations and features.

The business value of this Epic lies in its focus on minimizing downtime and preventing disruptions that could affect clients and end-users. It aligns with our commitment to delivering high-quality services and maintaining compliance with industry standards. Key deliverables include thorough audits of fee structures, updates to application components for compliance with current standards, and enhancements to user interfaces for improved accessibility and functionality.

The scope of this Epic includes a variety of tasks such as fee audits, application updates, and the integration of new features, which are necessary for optimizing the Adpay platform. Notable components include the Memoriams FH Fee Audit, updates to the PPN application, and enhancements for compliance with PCI standards. By addressing these areas, we will ensure that the Adpay platform remains competitive and responsive to user needs in 2026 and beyond.

item.title: [BLUE-921] Obitportal: User Facing Changes - 2026
item.description:
This epic focuses on expanding and refining the user-facing experience in Obitportal during FY2025 Q3. Enhancements target editorial flexibility, visual usability, and publication-specific configurations. Additional research spikes are included to support future roadmap features tied to order customization and intake integration.

Planned Work Includes:

  • Implement a deadline calendar for obituary placements to clearly communicate publication timing to users.
  • Enhance package description editing capabilities in the Admin Tool for more transparent user-facing copy.
  • Conduct research spikes on:
    • Complementary publication placement via partner networks.
    • Gannett-specific customization for confirmation emails and delivery logic.
    • Cross-platform intake unification across Obitportal and Adpay experiences.

CapEx Justification

This qualifies as Feature Work under GAAP ASC 350-40. Most items will introduce new or improved functionality within the customer experience. Spikes should be reviewed individually for capitalizability depending on follow-through implementation in future epics.

item.title: [BLUE-910] Customer Facing - Q1 2026
item.description:
Epic: Customer Experience Improvements & Enhancements

As a customer using our intake and publishing platforms
I want continuous improvements to the user interface, functionality, and overall experience
So that I can complete my tasks more easily, efficiently, and with greater satisfaction

Acceptance Criteria:

  • Changes directly improve the customer-facing experience (UI, functionality, performance, or usability)
  • Updates are documented and communicated appropriately to affected users
  • Changes are tested to ensure they don't negatively impact existing workflows
  • Improvements align with overall product strategy and user feedback

Examples of work included:

  • Minor UI/UX improvements and refinements
  • Small feature enhancements based on customer feedback
  • Bug fixes that impact customer experience
  • Copy or content updates that improve clarity
  • Performance optimizations visible to users
  • Accessibility improvements

item.title: [BLUE-909] Platform - Q1 2026
item.description:
*Epic Description: Platform - Q1 2026 (Key: BLUE-752 To Do )*

The Platform - Q1 2026 Epic aims to enhance the functionality and security of the software platform by addressing a diverse range of tasks and stories across various areas. The key objectives of this Epic include streamlining user management processes, improving system performance, ensuring data integrity, and enhancing user experience through feature enhancements and bug fixes. By implementing these tasks, the platform will be better equipped to support critical business operations and provide a more robust and reliable user experience.

The business value of this Epic lies in optimizing system operations, enhancing security measures, and improving user satisfaction. By addressing issues such as cache key collisions, security upgrades, date storage standardization, and error handling, the platform will be more resilient, secure, and efficient. Additionally, implementing features like export error alerts, responsive datatables, and admin tool enhancements will elevate the user experience and streamline workflow processes for both administrators and end-users.

Key deliverables of this Epic include resolving critical security vulnerabilities, enhancing system performance, improving data management practices, and introducing user-friendly features to enhance usability. The tasks and stories within this Epic cover a wide spectrum of technical areas, including security upgrades, bug fixes, feature enhancements, data standardization, and user experience improvements. By successfully completing these deliverables, the platform will be primed for Q1 2026 with enhanced functionality, improved security measures, and an overall better user experience.

item.title: [BLUE-907] Admin Facing - Q1 2026
item.description:
1. Epic Description: Admin Facing - Q1 2026 ( BLUE-907 Active )

The "Admin Facing - Q1 2026" Epic is designed to enhance the administrative capabilities of our platform, ensuring that admin users have the tools they need to efficiently manage content and user access. By implementing a series of targeted updates and improvements, this Epic aims to streamline administrative workflows, enhance user experience, and bolster the security of our authentication processes. Key objectives include the customization of messaging properties, improved authentication methods, and enhanced reporting functionalities, all of which contribute to a more robust and user-friendly backend system.

The scope of this Epic encompasses several key deliverables, including updates to the OP message properties feature, enhancements to the admin user authentication process via Okta, and the introduction of new functionalities like the "Place Obit" button. Additionally, it addresses critical issues such as the integration of AI-powered verification processes and the simplification of administrative tasks through features like cache clearing options. Collectively, these tasks are intended to create a cohesive and responsive administrative environment that supports the evolving needs of our users and improves operational efficiency.

By focusing on these enhancements in Q1 2026, we anticipate increased productivity for our admin users, a reduction in potential errors, and an overall improvement in the reliability and security of our platform.

item.title: [BLUE-744] Braze SDK Integration - AdPay & iPublish Components
item.description:
Objective

Integrate the Braze SDK with Legacy.com components to enhance campaign workflows currently managed by Salesforce Marketing Cloud (SFMC).

Context

This initiative aims to establish Braze as the primary customer engagement platform for Legacy, AdPay, and iPublish, replacing SFMC. It involves creating a modern data activation layer that connects various data sources for effective lifecycle messaging.

Scope

Included

  • Integrating the Braze SDK.
    • https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/getting_started/sdk_overview
  • Implementing form-based workflows in iPublish and AdPortal applications
  • Identifying and implementing data bridges to push data to Braze.
    • Re-working file drops as needed to get data available for Braze use cases
    • Technical implementation of needed APIs for connected CRM data in Braze
  • Connecting to available APIs for CRM campaign workflows.
    • https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1g10HPeF5o2E3A7--XyJUVG5Dee-vIMZs/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=116345858056203829785&rtpof=true&sd=true
  • Documenting data use cases that cannot be solved with out-of-the-box tooling.

Success criteria

  • Successful integration of the Braze SDK.
  • Data is pushed from user workflows to Braze.
  • All prioritized SFMC use cases are implemented.
  • Data bridges are established to facilitate data transfer to Braze.
  • Gaps in data are identified and documented.

Other information

  • Key Goals:
    1. Integrate SDK https://www.braze.com/docs/developer_guide/getting_started/sdk_overview
    2. PUSH data to Braze from user workflows.
    3. Integrate data for Braze Use cases.
  • Linked Information:
    • Issue: https://legacycom.atlassian.net/browse/INIT-183
    • SFMC Workflows: https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1g10HPeF5o2E3A7--XyJUVG5Dee-vIMZs/edit?usp=sharing&ouid=116345858056203829785&rtpof=true&sd=true
    • Key Deliverables include:
      • Implementation of Braze CRM and integration
      • Data pipelines for synchronizing data points and user profile attributes into Braze
item.summary: Braze SDK Integration - AdPay & iPublish Components

comments.comment: Joseph White not really clear yet on what we need to do here. I am guessing we need to use the REST API and not the javascript/client API. Has stitch provided a REST API example to replace the sending of emails from the the intake products? I know the abandon cart emails are a different animal, but the the other emails the data needed to send the email is in the request.

Jim Spurrell Nothing actionable on this epic yet, but we should have details soon.

Next steps:

  • Stitch team is providing some data recommendations. (I don’t know if it will be API recommendations, but it should include some data structure starting points.) (Expected today or next week.)
  • Emily + Danielle can recommend a campaign workflow that we can scope with Stitch and Spencer Heintz can help define requirements for that.
  • Then we should have some concrete tickets to work from.

In the meantime, I don’t think we’re totally blocked with sketching out our API implementation based on data we know we need from the segmentation data mapping here and documentation here: https://www.braze.com/docs/api/basics/?tab=user%20data

item.title: [BLUE-735] AI Content Verification
item.description:
The AI Content Verification Epic aims to establish an automated process for verifying the accuracy and quality of submitted obituaries, enhancing the editorial workflow for admin users. By leveraging artificial intelligence, this initiative seeks to streamline content validation, reduce manual review time, and ensure that all published obituaries meet established editorial guidelines. This automated approach not only improves operational efficiency but also boosts the reliability of the content presented to users, thereby enhancing user trust and engagement.

The project scope includes the development of key components such as the AI-based content verification endpoint specifically for obituaries. Deliverables will encompass a fully functional verification system that integrates seamlessly with existing editorial processes. Key tasks within this Epic involve the coding and testing of the verification algorithms, with current progress reflected in the ongoing code review and completed stories. By achieving these objectives, the Epic will significantly elevate the standard of obituary content while empowering admin users with effective tools for content management.

item.summary: Obit Portal - Remaining APIs for Unification

item.title: [BI-1025] Follower Reporting - 2026
item.description:
Epic Goal

Drive Follow Goals of +5,000 new subscribers 7 day average daily run rate by the end of January 2026.

Background & Context

  • Board meeting success created mandate for follower/known user growth as primary 2026 KPI.
  • Early evidence of positive impacts to OPN and Traffic from Obituary Follow
    • How do we optimize this even more?

Success Metrics

  • Increase in daily/weekly new email subscribers
  • Growth in follower counts across person pages and communities
  • Improved conversion rates on follow CTAs
  • Enhanced mobile engagement and conversion rates

Key Initiatives & Projects

Quick Wins (High Impact, Low Effort)

  1. CTA Button Optimization
    • Replace "Submit" buttons with "Follow" across all pages
    • Test copy variations: "Follow" vs "Notify me" vs "Get alerts" vs "Get updates"
    • More descriptive messaging about what users receive
  2. New Person Page Follow Enhancement
    • Move follow CTA higher in user journey
    • Add bookmark icons to related memorial pages
    • Implement "Get notified when there are new [Name] obituaries" prominence

Search & Discovery Features

  1. Saved Search MVP
    • Leverage existing by-name stack for search result following
    • Example: Search "Matarangus" → follow Matarangus updates
    • Use first/last name from query, allow user confirmation
  2. Related Content Integration
    • Feed integration after "most recent batch" of obituaries
    • Custom cards for notable events and date-driven content
    • Cross-promotion suggestions (city page model achieved 20-24% CTR historically)

Newspaper Partner Integration

  1. Community and Notable Deaths Placement on Newspaper Pages
    • Enable community links on newspaper affiliate pages
    • Revenue share codes already in place
    • Coordinate with Ernie for partner approval
  2. Email Collection Enhancement
    • Dual opt-in: community signup + newspaper notifications
    • Auto-inclusion for relevant content
    • Affiliate attribution for co-branded site signups

Mobile & UX Optimization

  1. Mobile Follow Experience with Exit Intent Optimization
    • Address 70% mobile traffic with desktop-only CTAs issue
    • Mobile-optimized follow flows
    • Exit intent popups for leaving users
  2. Social Proof Integration
    • Add follower counts where available
    • Year-to-date signup data from Salesforce
    • Enhanced value proposition messaging beyond basic email alerts

Technical Infrastructure

  1. Follow Integration for Consumer Obituaries
    • TuKios integration for unique IDs and slugs
    • Enable community data integration

Marketing & Acquisition

  1. Facebook Advertising Campaign
    • Micro-community targeting (local vs broad)
    • 10-15 creative pieces, $10-15/day budget
    • Focus on biggest geographic markets for audience size

Dependencies & Blockers

  • Ernie approval for newspaper community cards
  • Braze vs Salesforce integration decision for consumer obits
  • Partner negotiations for email sharing and attribution
  • Engineering bandwidth allocation

Team Allocation

  • Priority shift: Focus next 2-3 weeks on follower growth over other priorities.
  • Engineering: Lemon bandwidth available for quick wins, cause Lemon Sllllaaaayyyysss. Along w/ Supporting cast from Man/Paul where necessary.
